title: Won't Interpolate Last Frame
author: CopperCrusader
body:
Sometimes, Flowframes will interpolate everything but the last frame of a video.
For example, a 840 frame (14 sec) video from 60fps to 120fps (2x) will interpolate 1679 frames and skip the last 840th frame, causing it to show a black screen for the last two frames in the output. I have loop interpolation, de-duplication, and fix scene changes enabled (the disabling of which appear to not solve this) and I am using NCNN/VS with the 4.26 General Model.
Strangely, this issue seems to only affect specific videos and not others. I'm wondering if there's a fix for this. If not, I can only hope it will no longer be an issue in the next release.

title: NCNN GPU ID is not saved when you close the program.
author: Erosion139
body: If you set your GPU to say ID2 and close the program it will default back to 0.

title: Missing Python module
author: kiddrom881-blip
body: A python module named cv2 is missing in the "rife.py". After reviewing the same file for the indicated missing code on line 4 it still wont run.

title: Limit output framerate is not respected in export options (1.42.0)
author: Erosion139
body: If you set the output limit to 60fps and start a batch interpolation process it will not respect the output frame rate limit.

title: Custom RIFE models
author: Egor179
body: Is there a way to use custom RIFE models? When I replace the model file, the program checks it and downloads it again from the server; I need to use a custom "heavy" model

title: Intel QSV options are missing
author: igordovgaluk
body: Intel Quick Sync Video (QSV) could be made available for encoding. So interpolation and encoding could be done in parallel speeding up the process.

title: Texts're bugged in my video converted to 60 FPS using Flowframes software.
author: ghost
body:
When I export my video using FlowFrames and convert it to 60 FPS H264, texts (HUD elements) in my video’re bugued, as shown in the screenshot. I tried changing the output mode from MKV to MP4, then to H264 (fast) after trying H265, but the problem persists. Furthermore, the encoding process takes an extremely long time to create my video, which is only 13 seconds long. It takes 2 minutes (the same issue occurs with Kdenlive) to get this glitchy texts problem. Someone could please try to resolve this issue please ? 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/025c50d8-da40-4aa7-868c-fb5f12b91305)

title: RIFE NCNN VS choppy
author: ble400
body:
On 1.41.0 RC4, It does the whole interpolate process just fine and is faster than RIFE NCNN Non-VS, but the output file doesn't seem smooth like RIFE NCNN Non-VS is.

I've attached 2 files for a comparison between using RIFE NCNN Non-VS and VS.

RIFE NCNN Non-VS:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/705e5407-dc0d-402d-81a4-d4c6722b03d1

RIFE NCNN VS:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f5428e35-c0d9-429d-9243-483202dc5315


Both are interpolated by 8x for a 240fps video. No other changes besides that

Compare the two videos right about at the 2 second mark. The Non-VS has no stuttering but the VS one does.

title: vkAllocateMemory failed -2
author: ble400
body: Video got up to 67% with about 1,620,345 frames interpolated when all of the sudden said "MessageBox: Vulkan ran out of memory! [E] vkAllocateMemory failed -2 Try a lower resolution (Settings -> Max Video Size). (Error)" and tried to create the video but it's frozen. The input file is only 1080p... I have 32gb of RAM, an RTX 4070, and a Ryzen 9 5950x. Using the 1.41 RC4 version of Flowframes in the Patreon page

title: Feature Request: Allow custom python path in config instead of relying on PATH
author: ryq1212
body:
I already have Python and PyTorch installed on my system, and everything is set up properly. However, I deliberately did not add Python to the system PATH because I maintain multiple Python-based projects and want to avoid unnecessary conflicts.

At the same time, I would prefer not to use the embedded Python that comes with Flowframes, since it takes up additional space even though I already have fully functional environments.

Therefore, I’d like to suggest adding an option in the configuration to manually specify the path to `python.exe`, instead of relying solely on the Python version found in the system PATH. This would allow users like me to reuse an existing Python installation without installing the embedded one, while keeping multiple environments clean.
